evals.prompt: commit this
evals.expected_output: Stages changes, composes conventional commit, creates commit. No explanatory text.
expectations: Runs git status and git diff to understand current changes
Stages relevant files with git add
Commit message starts with a valid conventional commit type (feat, fix, chore, etc.)
Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary

evals.prompt: ok looks good, commit it
evals.expected_output: Uses conversation context instead of redundant git commands. Creates commit with correct type.
evals.context: The assistant just finished adding a new search bar component with debounce filtering to the dashboard page.
expectations: Skips git diff and git log since the assistant already knows what changed from conversation context
May run git status to confirm staging, but skips full diff analysis
Commit message starts with 'feat:' since a new feature was added
Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary

evals.prompt: I fixed the race condition in the websocket handler where messages could arrive before the connection was fully initialized
evals.expected_output: Commit with fix: prefix and concise description.
expectations: Commit message starts with 'fix:' since this is a bug fix
Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Description captures the essence of the fix concisely — does not repeat the entire user prompt
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary

Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary

evals.prompt: commit these changes. I moved all the validation logic from the controllers into a shared middleware layer. No behavior changes.

Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Does not use 'feat:' even though new files may have been created — user explicitly said no behavior changes
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary

evals.prompt: commit. this removes the deprecated /api/v1 endpoints — it's a breaking change
evals.expected_output: Commit with ! breaking change indicator and/or BREAKING CHANGE footer.
expectations: Commit message includes '!' after type/scope (e.g., 'feat(api)!:') and/or includes a 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer
Commit description is lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period
Does not include a session link in the commit message
Only emits tool calls — no explanatory text or commentary
